item.title: Bacchus Wood Line Ash4/E Vintage White 【4.11kg】 — $957.87
item.description:
$957.87 · Brand New · Yamanashi Musical Instrument Store

The Bacchus Wood Line ASH4/E is a premium Japanese-made Jazz Bass that combines outstanding craftsmanship, versatile electronics, and excellent playability.

Built in 2024, this Wood Line model features an ash body, maple neck, and ebony fingerboard. The ash body delivers a bright, punchy response with excellent clarity, while the ebony fingerboard adds definition and fast attack.

Equipped with two Jazz Bass-style pickups, the bass offers classic JB tones ranging from warm, rounded sounds to articulate modern bass tones. A coil-tap function further expands its versatility, allowing players to achieve brighter, more single-coil-like tones when desired.

The transparent pickguard complements the Vintage White finish, giving the instrument a clean and modern appearance while allowing more of the body to remain visible.

Weighing 4.11kg, the bass is well balanced and comfortable for rehearsals, recording sessions, and live performances.

Approximately 80% of the fret life remains, with a comfortable setup measuring 2.0mm on the treble side and 2.5mm on the bass side at the 12th fret.

Cosmetically, the bass shows slight tarnishing on some metal hardware, but otherwise has very few scratches or signs of wear and remains in excellent overall condition.

Specifications

Body: Ash

Neck: Maple

Fingerboard: Ebony

Neck Joint: Bolt-On

Controls: 2 Volume, 1 Tone, Coil Tap

Pickup Configuration: JB Type

Color: Vintage White

Frets: 21

Weight: 4.11kg

Condition

Made in 2024.

Transparent pickguard.

Coil-tap function.

Approximately 80% fret life remaining.

Action at the 12th fret: 2.0mm (1st string), 2.5mm (4th string).

Minor tarnishing on metal hardware.

Excellent overall condition.

item.title: Fender Jaco Pastorius Jazz Bass [4.24kg][2023s] — $1,932.60
item.description:
$1,932.60 · Very Good · Kurosawa Music Ochanomizu

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.




Jaco Pastorius—a legendary bassist who can truly be called a legend.


The image that remains etched in our minds as his iconic symbol is, without a doubt, the fretless Fender Jazz Bass.


Countless bassists admire and idolize him, and it’s no longer uncommon to see them pay homage by modifying even standard Sunburst Jazz Basses to be fretless or by removing the pickguard.




The signature model we’ve just received is one we’d highly recommend to bassists all over the world.


Released by Fender USA with absolutely no compromises, this model faithfully reproduces Jaco’s beautiful tone as closely as possible.


It features a carefully selected alder body, a C-shaped maple neck, and a pearwood fingerboard.


It’s equipped with an American Vintage Single-Coil Jazz Bass pickup.


A sound that’s raw, light, yet heavy—one that simply makes you want to keep playing forever. It all comes from this single instrument.


Please come and experience it for yourself.






As this is a used item, it has some expected wear and tear, but it remains in relatively good condition.


There is plenty of truss rod adjustment left, and there are currently no issues with the electronics.




Weight: Approx. 4.10 kg


Included Accessories: Original hard case, pickguard, finger rest, pickup fence, bridge cover


Translated with DeepL.com (free version)




*Please note that there may be some minor scratches or stains on the body and accessories for B grade new, Outlet New, and New Special Price items.

item.title: Lakland Skyline 44-01 Bass — $1,075.68
item.description:
$1,075.68 · Excellent · Noel

Up for sale is my Lakland Skyline 44-01 in excellent condition. Lakland's Skyline line is made in Korea to the same specs Dan Lakin uses in Chicago, and it shows in the build quality and playability.


The finish is a gloss black. No dings, scratches, or finish checking. Hardware and frets are in great shape.

Specs:

- Body: Alder with flame maple top, gloss black finish

- Neck: Maple, bolt-on

- Fretboard: Rosewood

- Pickups: Bartolini MK-1 soapbar (neck & bridge)

- Preamp: Active 3-band EQ (Fishman)

- Bridge: Lakland/Hipshot-style with individual saddles

- Tuners: Lakland 4-in-line, sealed


The active preamp gives this bass excellent low-end definition and clarity. Action is comfortable, neck is straight. Plays great as-is, no setup needed.


Includes a Lakland hard case


Happy to answer questions or provide additional photos.

item.title: JMP2B-ASH-M -Open Pore Black-【4.07kg】 — $1,141.40
item.description:
$1,141.40 · Brand New · GUITAR PLANET

“FGN” is a long-established brand renowned worldwide for its precision manufacturing technology and consistent quality. We are pleased to introduce the “JMP2B-ASH-M,” a modern precision bass born from the brand’s proven craftsmanship.

The body is crafted from ash, delivering a clear, wide-ranging tone with deep, resonant bass. The combination of a maple neck and maple fingerboard produces a well-defined, projective sound that stands out even within an ensemble.

The frets feature FGN’s proprietary “Circle Fretting System (C.F.S.).” Thanks to the arc-shaped fret structure, the strings always make contact at a right angle, ensuring accurate pitch and a stable tone. It accommodates a wide range of playing styles, from delicate phrases to powerful riffs.

It is equipped with two split-coil pickups. While centered on the powerful bass tones of the traditional Precision Bass, it allows you to draw out a diverse range of sounds through simple controls: two volume knobs and one tone knob. It effortlessly covers everything from a fat, punchy tone to a midrange with a strong presence.

The FGN “JMP2B-ASH-M” combines practicality with a sophisticated look. This Japanese-made modern Precision bass delivers reliable performance from the stage to the studio. We invite you to take this opportunity to consider it.

SPEC
Body: Ash
Neck: Maple
Neck Scale: 34"
Fingerboard: Maple
Number of Fret: 22,Medium Jumbo C.F.S.
Pickups: Seymour Duncan Quarter-Pound SPB-3
Control: 2Vol,1Tone
Case: Soft Case
Weight: 4.07kg
Serial Number:G260084

---------------------------------------
Thank you for checking GUITAR PLANET.

-Make sure to log on to PAYPAL for ordering.
-All items are tracked and insured.

Items may sold at storefront before changing status.
Buyer is responsible for import fee if applied.
---------------------------------------

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass JB-40 1994 - Fiesta red — $820
item.description:
$820 · Very Good · Easy Guitars

Stylish 1994 Fender Jazz Bass JB-40 in Fiesta Red, freshly imported from Japan.

The bass has been fully inspected and serviced by our workshop. It has a fresh set of Gotoh tuners installed, the truss rod has plenty of adjustment room, and the frets have been leveled, dressed and polished. The setup is comfortable, with low string action and smooth playability across the neck.

Cosmetically, the bass is in good used condition for its age. There are some small dents, chips and signs of wear around the edges, as shown in the photos, but nothing that affects playability.

A great-looking vintage-style Japanese Jazz Bass with proper workshop setup and reliable hardware.

Worldwide shipping available. Please message first to calculate shipping.

item.title: HONEY (EX-TEISCO) SEB-30 1969 - BLACK — $1,005.21
item.description:
$1,005.21 · Very Good · VEJ Guitars

TEISCO HONEY SEB-30 - VINTAGE JAPAN 1960s - RARE SCROLL HEAD VIOLIN BASS

With its larger hollow body and full scroll head the Honey SEB-30 is one of the coolest violin basses to come out of Japan. This one was made around 1968-69.

Formed by mostly ex-Teisco artisans after the Kawai takeover of Teisco in 1967, Honey produced some very high quality instruments in its brief existence.

These are very rare now.


Condition-wise...

Very good.

The body has surface lacquer cracks but these are purely superficial, not structural. There are a number of small touch-ins to the body and head. (See photos)

The neck is in super condition.

Bindings to body and neck are fine.

Fretboard is in great shape and the frets have plenty of life.


It's completely original save for the addition of a control panel on the back. This is actually a very worthwhile modification since the design of the top bracing makes it difficult to work inside.

Hardware, pickups, controls, pick guard - all original and working fine. Some of the chrome parts are a little tarnished.

The original floating rosewood bridge (complete with damping foam) and tailpiece are in very good order too.


We just got this back from a full pro set up.

The electrics were overhauled and are now perfect. We also installed a new nut and zero fret so fingering and intonation are very good.

The action is very comfortable and it plays very well indeed.


These have a superb vintage thump to them.

In our estimation, the Honey is the best-sounding of the 1960s Golden Era Japanese violin basses. Absolutely gorgeous tone.

Plus they look so cool.



Overall...

A very rare Golden Era Japan violin bass with a fine ex-Teisco pedigree.

Fabulous vintage looks and very cool and authentic 60s tones.

Comes with a very nice quality thickly padded semi-hard case.


Specs:

  • HONEY (TEISCO) SEB-30 *RARE* 1960s MIJ SCROLL HEAD VIOLIN BASS
  • Hollow double-bound violin body with twin F-holes in original black finish
  • Bolt-on bound slim maple neck
  • Nut 41mm  (New)
  • Large Slotted scroll headstock
  • Rosewood fretboard w/Block inlays - 22 frets incl (new) 'Zero Fret'
  • 78.5cm / 31" Scale
  • Original Honey pickups w/'Batwing' escutcheons
  • Original Floating Rosewood bridge with double screw height adjustment
  • Controls: 1 x V; 1 x T; 3-position toggle selector
  • Weight: 2.8kg / 6.2lbs

    *** WORLDWIDE SHIPPING AVAILABLE ***

Please contact us for a quote if your location is not listed.


Australia, Germany, Spain & Denmark Buyers:

Unfortunately the post office in these countries now limits package sizes so our regular EMS (Japan Post Office) shipping rates do not apply. (Unless we ship it disassembled). Please contact us for a quote.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Almost everything for sale here is coming from a 40+years personal collection. As the shop name suggests these are predominantly Vintage, Eclectic or Japanese - so if you are looking for something specific, please get in touch. Maybe we have got something you've been searching for. VEJ

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A Note On Shipping:

We want you to be fully satisfied with everything - from the item itself (naturally!) through the shipping, packaging, communication etc.

Of course there is nothing we can do about customs duty, local taxes etc at your end. But we can keep the overall price as competitive as is decently possible.

The Japan post office service (EMS) is reliable and efficient but shipping costs from Japan to Europe or the states are unavoidably expensive.

For this reason none of the shipping costs we quote reflect the actual price we have to pay. We are more than willing to take a hit on that to make things as attractive for you as possible.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Vintage Instruments & Equipment

Shipping by air plus climatic changes can affect how a guitar is set up - no matter how well it is packed. Consequently there is no guarantee that an instrument arrives with exactly the same set up as stated. After a period of acclimatisation, you may still need to make any adjustments at your own expense.

item.title: Ibanez SR600 - Finition Bois Naturel - 2010 - Excellent État - Entièrement Révisée — $597.60
item.description:
$597.60 · Excellent · old school and vintage guitars

Ibanez SR600 - Natural Finish - 2010 - Excellent Condition - Fully Set Up

For sale: a beautiful Ibanez SR600 bass, made in 2010, featuring a stunning Natural Flat wood finish. This model is known for its high-quality craftsmanship, tonal versatility, and exceptional playing comfort, true to the reputation of the Soundgear (SR) series.

✅ Key Features:

  • Body: Lightweight ash with a satin natural wood finish
  • Neck: 5-piece jatoba/bubinga, slim and fast profile
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood, 24 frets
  • Pickups: Bartolini MK1 (neck and bridge)
  • Electronics: Active 3-band EQ with active/passive switch
  • Made in Indonesia (2010)

item.title: Fender Japan JB-62 3Tone Sunburst 【4.14㎏】 — $778.23
item.description:
$778.23 · Brand New · Yamanashi Musical Instrument Store

The Fender Japan JB-62 is a classic Jazz Bass inspired by the iconic 1962 specification, offering the authentic feel and tone that have made the Jazz Bass a favorite among players for decades.

Produced around 2006–2008, this model features an alder body, maple neck, and rosewood fingerboard, delivering warm lows, punchy mids, and clear highs that suit virtually any musical style.

Equipped with two traditional Jazz Bass single-coil pickups, the JB-62 offers a wide tonal range. By blending the individual pickup volumes, you can easily dial in everything from warm vintage tones to articulate, cutting sounds.

Weighing 4.14kg, the bass provides excellent balance and is equally at home on stage, in the studio, or at rehearsal.

Approximately 70% of the fret life remains, with a comfortable setup measuring 2.0mm on the treble side and 2.5mm on the bass side at the 12th fret.

Please note that the front pickup cover has a crack, and the mounting screws have been replaced with non-original hardware. These cosmetic issues do not affect the instrument's playability.

Overall, the bass remains in typical condition for its age.

Specifications

Body: Alder

Neck: Maple

Fingerboard: Rosewood

Neck Joint: Bolt-On

Controls: 2 Volume

Pickup Configuration: JB Type

Color: 3-Tone Sunburst

Frets: 20

Weight: 4.14kg

Condition

Made around 2006–2008.

Cracked front pickup cover.

Non-original pickup mounting screws.

Approximately 70% fret life remaining.

Action at the 12th fret: 2.0mm (1st string), 2.5mm (4th string).

Overall condition is typical for its age.

item.description:

With its robust bass and natural response, this instrument truly brings out the signature presence of a Precision Bass.

Dating from the 2012 American Standard era, this model features Custom Shop f60s-style pickups, delivering a character that leans more toward vintage tones than previous models while maintaining a solid, well-defined low end.

The stable construction?featuring an alder body, a maple neck, and graphite reinforcement?ensures quick attack and stable pitch. The satin-finished back of the neck offers a smooth feel, while the High Mass Vintage-style bridge produces rich sustain and just the right amount of tension.

While it possesses the characteristic thickness of a Precision Bass, the midrange remains clear and well-defined, ensuring the sound retains its distinct contour even within an ensemble.
There are some minor scratches and dents, but no particularly noticeable damage.

item.title: HOFNER 500/1 - 1973-4 w/HARD CASE - ORIGINAL VINTAGE VIOLIN BASS — $2,594.10
item.description:
$2,594.10 · Very Good · VEJ Guitars

Vintage Hofner 500/1 Violin Bass c.1973/1974 with Hard Case


This is an all-original 500/1 from 1973-4. It's in super shape all round.

No damage, modifications or repairs.


The back is in beautiful condition, the neck is spotless and all the bindings are very clean.

Fret wear is minimal.

Some very minor scrapes to the headstock corners and some fairly typical lacquer hairlines to the top. These feel smooth to the touch and are purely cosmetic. (See photos).


Original hardware.

Aside from some age-related tarnishing to the chrome, nothing to report.

Tuners are smooth and precise and the electrics are faultless.



It's had a full pro set up. It feels, sounds and plays beautifully.

Comes complete with the good quality hard case pictured.


Specs:

  • Hollow double bound Spruce/Maple violin body in original finish
  • C-shaped maple neck with bound rosewood fingerboard / 30" scale 
  • Black side dots w/pearl dot inlays
  • Bound headstock w/black overlay and gold Hofner logo
  • Tuners closed type w/plastic buttons
  • Hofner embossed 'Blade' pickups 
  • Black control knobs w/bass, treble, rhythm/solo toggle switches
  • Trapezoidal tailpiece / ebony bridge / floating rosewood plinth
  • Pearloid scratchplate w/black Hofner logo 
  • Pro setup w/La Bella Beatle bass flat wound strings
  • Nut: 42mm / 1 5/8"  
  • Hard case as shown

item.title: ARIA DIAMOND 1720 BASS - c.1970 w/HSC - UNIVOX HI-FLIER PHASE II - MATSUMOKU BUILD — $1,164.10
item.description:
$1,164.10 · Very Good · VEJ Guitars

ARAI / ARIA DIAMOND 1720 - UNIVOX HI-FLIER PHASE II VINTAGE BASS w/HSC *RARE*


This is the Arai Diamond 1720 or (U.S.) Phase II Univox Hi-Flier bass made by Matsumoku somewhere around 1970.

A vintage MIJ offset bass that is fast-becoming rare and collectable.


This Phase II version has a selector switch as opposed to the earlier rockers. Apart from this and minor cosmetic changes it's pretty much the same as the Phase I.

Best of all it retains these fantastic P90-type pickups which are actually powerful single coils and really full and dynamic.

The sound and the feel of these basses helps them punch way above their weight.



Condition-wise...

Close to excellent.

The neck is almost spotless. The body has no modifications, cracks or repairs with the only visible wear to the black edges. Quite hard to show in the photos.

Also there are some minor splits to the finish around the neck pocket - purely cosmetic - and minor rubs to the headstock.

(Please check out the photos)


Fretboard is great, frets are good and hardware is all-original - pickups, controls, tuners, bridge and bridge cover are all present and correct. Complete with some mute foam still attached.


It plays beautifully. Set up with flat wound strings. And it sounds brilliant.


In Summary...

Matsumoku golden-era build quality, fantastic pickups and a lovely fast and comfortable feel.

An increasingly rare and collectable model in super clean condition. As Japanese short-scale basses go, these are pretty close to the benchmark.

Comes complete with a decent vintage hard case.


Specs:

  • Arai Diamond 1720 bass / (U.S.) Univox Hi-Flier Phase II c.1970 - Matsumoku build
  • Mahogany body in original sunburst finish
  • Maple neck (30" scale)
  • Rosewood fingerboard (20 frets) w/dot markers
  • Original hardware
  • Original scratch plate
  • Original Controls: 1 x Volume; 1 x Tone; 3-way selector
  • Vintage hard case included (Pictured)

item.title: Black Smoker STD BETA J5 XL -Black/MH- [4.54kg] — $2,485.47
item.description:
$2,485.47 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

-----

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.

-----

Welcome to

Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our

shop policy below the guitar description!

For Paypal payments, please register your address in your Paypal account in advance.

------------------------------------------


The model we’ve just received is the Standard Series BETA 5-string Jazz Bass.


Weighing in at 4.54 kg—standard for a 5-string bass—this is an excellent instrument with standout low-to-midrange tones from its alder body and rosewood fingerboard, plus a smooth, extended sustain!

It features a combination of five-string tuners and a matching headstock, which is particularly popular even within this manufacturer’s lineup. It also features a 34.5-inch scale length, which is slightly longer than that of a standard bass.



True to the classic combination of an alder body and rosewood fingerboard, the tone is warm,

yet it’s tight with a quick attack, giving the impression that it responds firmly to the player’s dynamics.

Although it’s a passive bass, the mini-switch allows you to toggle between series and parallel modes, so you can maintain tightness and tonal definition while still achieving a sound with enough presence to hold its own against other instruments and the bass drum.



Thanks to the 34.5-inch scale, the string tension feels just right compared to a standard 5-string bass,

and since the bridge allows for under-saddle string routing, you can customize the setup to your personal preference.

These kinds of thoughtful details may be the very reason this model is loved by so many artists.



It’s a bass that lets you appreciate its qualities the moment you play it, so if you’re interested, don’t wait—get yours soon!



【About Black Smoker】


Led by Mr. Kuroiwa, one of Japan’s top guitar builders, and Mr. Yanagisawa, a master builder in whom he places immense trust—and now joined by one additional member—this brand operates with an ultra-select, elite team. True to the motto “quality over quantity,” monthly production numbers are modest, but the appeal of this Japanese brand lies in the superb craftsmanship of each instrument, ensuring every single one is a standout.


They always prioritize the player’s perspective. For example, they’ve replaced the bridge saddle’s set screws—which can protrude and make playing uncomfortable—with a different type.

The truss rod adjustment hole is positioned on the headstock so it’s easy to adjust even when the guitar is standing upright on a stand. This placement allows for immediate adjustments if the neck’s condition changes during a performance due to stage lighting or sweaty hands.



【Spec】

Body：Alder　
Neck：Maple　
Fingerboard：Indian Rosewood
Neck Shape：C Shape
Scale：876mm/21F
Nut Width：Bone / 48mm
Radius：305R
Pickups：BLACK SMOKER
Contorol：2Vol,1Tone,Mini SW (Series/Parallel)
Machinehead：GOTOH GB528
Bridge：GOTOH 404BO-5
Pickguard：4-Ply Brown Tortoise
Finish：Urethane


Accessories: Gig Bag

item.title: 【new】HEX Guitars / RB100R S/BK【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $755
item.description:
$755 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

HEX Guitars is a South Korea-based brand specializing in entry-level to mid-range guitars.

They own their own factory in Indonesia and manufacture their guitars themselves. This sets them apart from brands that typically use OEM manufacturing.

Their passion for production, with the boss himself involved in the process, allows them to produce guitars of a surprisingly high quality for their price point.

The B series features models that blend classic character with modern specifications.

The RB100 is a modern 4-string PJ model.

The body is made of Indonesian Mahogany.

Indonesian Mahogany is highly valued in recent years for its weight and tone, which are well-suited to musical instruments.

The stable supply of such high-quality materials in Indonesia makes it one of the most logical locations for high-quality instrument manufacturing.

The neck is made of Maple.

Two carbon fiber rods are incorporated to enhance its strength.

The fretboard is made of Indian rosewood, and the fretboard radius is a relatively flat 12 inches.

The neck has a thin, easy-to-grip C-shape.

The scale length is the most common 34” scale.

The pickups are original HEX® Alnico Single Coil pickups.

They use original pickups in many of their models.

This isn't for cost-cutting, but because they have confidence in the quality of their self-designed pickups.

The staff, who understand music and play it regularly, thoroughly research many elements such as magnets, wires, and bobbins before designing them.

What you'll notice when you hold it is its high playability.

It's extremely easy to play and stress-free.

The sound is characterized by a thick attack typical of PJ style guitars and a full, warm, and sustained midrange.

In an ensemble, it provides a strong, solid bottom end with a definite presence.

The high quality of the basic neck components contributes to its excellent playability, and it's difficult to say that it's inferior to more expensive instruments.

Overall, it's a superb product with high quality in looks, playability, and sound.

●SPECS

BODY
MATERIAL: Indonesian Mahogany
SHAPE: HEX® RB
FINISH: Satin

NECK
MATERIAL: Maple

SCALE: 864mm(34")

The model we’ve just received is the BETA J4—

a classic jazz bass featuring an alder body and a rosewood fingerboard, finished in “Faded Sonic Blue”!

Its pale blue hue, with a slightly muted vintage feel that looks as if it’s been well-played over the years, really makes you want to own it!

With its matching headstock, the overall look feels very refreshing—perfect for the coming summer.


As expected from the classic combination of an alder body and rosewood fingerboard, the tone is warm,

yet it’s tight with a quick attack, giving the impression that it responds firmly to the player’s dynamics.

Although it’s a passive bass, the mini-switch allows you to toggle between series and parallel modes, so you can maintain that tightness and clarity while still achieving a sound with enough presence to hold its own against other instruments and the bass drum.



It’s so cute-looking you’ll want to buy it on the spot, but when you play it, you’ll be surprised by how well-balanced the volume is across each string and how well it cuts through—making it the perfect choice for a band bassist! Be sure to give it a try!




【About Black Smoker】


Led by Mr. Kuroiwa, one of Japan’s top guitar builders, and Mr. Yanagisawa, a master builder in whom he places immense trust—and now joined by one additional member—this brand operates with an ultra-select, elite team. True to the motto “quality over quantity,” monthly production numbers are modest, but the appeal of this Japanese brand lies in the superb craftsmanship of each instrument, ensuring every single one is a standout.


They always prioritize the player’s perspective. For example, they’ve replaced the bridge saddle’s set screws—which can protrude and make playing uncomfortable—with a different type.

The truss rod adjustment hole is positioned on the headstock so it’s easy to adjust even when the guitar is standing upright on a stand. This placement allows for immediate adjustments if the neck’s condition changes during a performance due to stage lighting or sweaty hands.



【Spec】

Body Wood: Alder
Neck Wood: Maple
F.Board Wood: Indian Rosewood
Neck Shape: C Shape
Scale: 864mm / 21F
Nut / Width: Bone / 38mm
Fret: Mediom Jumbo NS
Radius: 305R
Machinehead: GOTOH GB528
Bridge: GOTOH 404BO-4
Pickguard: 3-Ply Parchment
Pickups: BLACK SMOKER Original
Electronics: Vol, Vol, Tone, Mini SW(Series/Parallel)
Finish: Urethane


Accessories: Gig Bag

item.title: Black Smoker STD BETA J5 XL -3 Tone Sunburst- [4.47kg] — $2,383.33
item.description:
$2,383.33 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

-----

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.

-----

Welcome to

Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our

shop policy below the guitar description!

For Paypal payments, please register your address in your Paypal account in advance.

------------------------------------------



The model we’ve just received is the BETA J5 XL.

It’s a classic Jazz Bass featuring an alder body and a rosewood fingerboard, finished in the classic 3-tone sunburst!

Unusually for a sunburst finish, the sides of the body are finished in brown rather than black.



Weighing in at 4.47 kg—a comfortable weight for a 5-string bass—it delivers the rich mid-to-low frequencies characteristic of alder and rosewood, with a smooth, extended sustain.



In addition to its five-string tuners, it features a 34.5-inch scale length, which is slightly longer than that of a standard bass.

As a result, it offers a more balanced string tension compared to a typical 5-string bass, and since the bridge allows for under-saddle string routing, you can customize the setup to your personal preference.

These thoughtful details may be the reason it’s loved by so many artists.



As expected from the classic combination of an alder body and rosewood fingerboard, the sound is warm,

yet it’s tight with a fast attack, giving the impression that it responds firmly to the player’s dynamics.

Although it’s a passive bass, the mini-switch allows you to toggle between series and parallel modes, so you can maintain tightness and clear articulation while still producing a sound with enough presence to hold its own against other instruments and the bass drum.



Featuring the classic Jazz Bass color, five-in-line tuners, a 34.5-inch scale, through-body wiring, and even a series/parallel switch, this is truly a fully-equipped bass. Be sure to give it a try!


【Spec】

Body : Alder　
Neck : Maple　
Fingerboard : Indian Rosewood
Neck Shape:C Shape
Scale: 876mm/21F
Nut Width : Bone / 48mm
Radius: 305R
Fret : Medium Jumbo NS
Pickups : BLACK SMOKER
Contorol : 2Vol,1Tone,Mini SW (Series/Parallel)
Machinehead:GOTOH GB528
Bridge:GOTOH 404BO-5
Pickguard : 4-Ply Tortoiseshell
Finish : Urethane

Accessories: Gig Bag

item.title: 【used】Fodera / Emperor 5 Standard Special 4.175kg #E5SS205【Consignment】【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $10,712
item.description:
$10,712 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fodera is one of the world's most popular high-end bass guitar brands.

Founder Vinny Fodera began his career in his teens.

After working at top workshops such as Spector and Ken Smith, he founded Fodera Guitars in 1983.

Top bass players worldwide, including Victor Wooten, Richard Bona, and Anthony Jackson, use their instruments. (Titles omitted)

This is the Emperor model, a relatively classic style within the Fodera lineup.

With its double-cutaway body, its familiar body shape has made it popular with many bassists.

This particular model is a special one, featuring a quilted maple top.

Manufactured in 2021.

As mentioned, the body top is quilted maple, and the body back is alder.

Finished with a clear satin paint, it boasts a beautiful finish that highlights the wood grain and texture.

The neck is made of maple, and the fingerboard is made of Indian rosewood.

Despite the use of a top wood, the overall construction gives a traditional impression.

The pickups are original dual-coils, and the preamp is also an original Fodera unit.

A combination that's sure to impress.

With its rich harmonics and silky texture, yet possessing powerful lows and a sustained midrange, this sound is truly versatile and matches any ensemble.

This is the latest model from a high-end bass brand, and we highly recommend you try it.

The frets have approximately 90% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and finish are generally clean.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume/Master Tone, Balancer, Treble/Bass, Middle, Active/Passive Switch, Dual Pickup Coil Tap Switch
Nut Width: 47.0mm
Bridge String Spacing: 20.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 2021
Country of Manufacture: USA

This guitar will be delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by a GIB craftsman.

This is a consignment item, so shipping costs may differ from the usual.

Accessories: Original hardshell case, spec sheet, wrench

Text: Tomoki Hayakawa

Inspection 담당: Oshima

Product Photos: Tei

GIB 3-Month Warranty Included

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free "String Vibration Setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee the repurchase price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. Excluding some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.

The warranty period is 3 months for used items and 1 year for new items.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

*Malfunctions and damages caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.

* If the warranty certificate is not presented or is lost.

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you purchase a new instrument, you will receive a 10% discount on the sales price when you use our trade-in service. (Consignment items are excluded. This discount applies only if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the sales price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We also ship internationally.

item.title: 【new】HEX Guitars / B100M G/MFG【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $703
item.description:
$703 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

HEX Guitars is a South Korea-based brand specializing in entry-level to mid-range guitars.

They own their own factory in Indonesia and manufacture their guitars themselves. This sets them apart from brands that typically use OEM manufacturing.

Their passion is so strong that the boss himself is involved in the production process, resulting in guitars of a quality far exceeding what one might expect from their price point.

The B series features models that blend classic character with modern specifications.

This B100 is a modern 4-string JB model.

The body is made of Indonesian Mahogany.

Indonesian Mahogany is highly valued in recent years for its weight and tone, which are well-suited to musical instruments.

The stable supply of such high-quality wood in Indonesia makes it one of the most logical locations for high-quality instrument manufacturing.

The neck is made of Maple.

Two carbon fiber rods are incorporated to enhance its strength.

The fretboard is also made of maple, and the fretboard radius is a relatively flat 12 inches.

The neck has a thin C-shape profile that is easy to grip.

The scale length is the most common 34” scale.

The pickup is the original HEX® Alnico Single Coil.

They use original pickups in many of their models.

This isn't for cost-cutting, but because they have confidence in the quality of their self-designed pickups.

The staff, who understand music and play it regularly, thoroughly research many elements such as magnets, wires, and bobbins before designing.

What you'll notice when you hold it is its high playability.

It's very easy to play and stress-free.

The sound is characterized by a fast attack and clear, well-defined high frequencies.

In an ensemble, it provides a strong bottom end with a sharp, defined tone.

The high quality of the basic neck components contributes to its excellent playability, and it's difficult to say that it's inferior to more expensive instruments.

Overall, it's a superb product with high quality in looks, playability, and sound.

●SPECS

BODY
MATERIAL: Indonesian Mahogany
SHAPE: HEX® B
FINISH: Gloss

NECK
MATERIAL: Maple
SHAPE : "C"
SCALE: 864mm(34")
TRUSS ROD : Spoke Nut Type
ASSEMBLY : 4 Bolt Steel Square Plate

FRET BOARD
MATERIAL: Maple
FINISH: Light Matte
FRETS: 21, Nickel
RADIUS: 12"
NUT: Bone
INLAY : Pearloid Dot, 6mm

HEAD
SHAPE : HEX® Original
TUNERS : Vintage Open Gear

PICKUPS
CONFIGURATION : Single + Single
CONTROLS: 2-Volume, 1-Tone
NECK PICKUP : HEX® Alnico Single Coil
BRIDGE PICKUP : HEX® Alnico Single Coil

HARDWARE
BRIDGE : Vintage Type 4-Saddle
STRING: Nickel Plated Steel (.045-.105 Gauges)
CASE: Deluxe Gigbag
